# WI-QUIC-CRT-0023 - QUIC CRT server chain-policy callback path work item

## Summary

Honor server-side CertificateChainPolicy on the existing callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path by delegating presented-client-certificate evaluation to BCL X509 chain primitives without widening broader policy promises.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0125

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0023

## Planned Changes

- Admit CertificateChainPolicy on the existing callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path while keeping standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode rejected.
- Thread the selected server SslServerAuthenticationOptions into the runtime/bridge so the callback seam can build an X509Chain for the presented client certificate.
- Clone the supplied CertificateChainPolicy into that chain, build the chain, compute SslPolicyErrors, and invoke the existing callback with the built chain.
- Fail closed on trust or policy mismatch when the callback does not override acceptance.
- Add focused positive and negative requirement-home tests for policy acceptance, trust mismatch failure, missing client certificate failure, callback-driven acceptance regression, and unchanged reject-first behavior for standalone revocation-mode customization.

## Out of Scope

- public listener widening
- standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode support
- broad PKI or path-building integration
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- 0-RTT
- key update
- broader client-auth policy beyond the callback-driven CertificateChainPolicy floor

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for the server chain-policy floor and the existing callback-driven floor, re-run the current CRT/API/INT guards that must remain unchanged, re-run the full REQ_QUIC_CRT_ sweep, and run repo-local SpecTrace validation/render checks after the canonical JSON updates.

## Completion Notes

This work item lands the smallest honest server-side policy follow-on on the existing SslServerAuthenticationOptions carrier: CertificateChainPolicy on the callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path. Standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode, broader PKI/path building, 0-RTT, key update, transfer, retry, and broader client-auth remain out of scope.
